Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate renal dysfunction or possible urinary tract pathology. Hemoglobin A1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.
